sections.title: TCS Overview
sections.content:
Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is an Indian multinational IT services, consulting, and BPS company headquartered in Mumbai, India. It is a subsidiary of Tata Sons and part of the Tata Group.

Founded in 1968 by J.R.D. Tata. First GM: F.C. Kohli (Father of Indian IT Industry). First software export in 1969 to Burroughs Corporation, USA.

Key Facts:
- Founded: 1968 | HQ: Mumbai, India
- Parent: Tata Sons | Type: Public Limited Company
- Listed on: BSE (532540) and NSE (TCS)
- Employees: 6,00,000+ | Countries: 55+ | Delivery Centers: ~150
- Revenue: ~$29.1 billion (FY2024) | Net Profit: ~$5.2 billion

Leadership (2024):
- CEO & MD: K. Krithivasan (since June 2023)
- CFO: Samir Seksaria | CHRO: Milind Lakkad
- COO: N. Ganapathy Subramaniam
- Chairman of Tata Sons: N. Chandrasekaran (ex-TCS CEO)

Previous CEOs: F.C. Kohli → S. Ramadorai → N. Chandrasekaran (2009–17) → Rajesh Gopinathan (2017–23) → K. Krithivasan (2023–present)

Revenue by Geography:
- North America: ~53% (Largest) | Europe: ~31% | India: ~5%

Top Industry Verticals:
1. BFSI — ~32% (Largest)
2. Retail & Consumer | 3. CMT | 4. Manufacturing
5. Life Sciences & Healthcare | 6. Energy & Utilities

Service Lines: IT Services, Consulting, BPS, Engineering, Cloud, AI & Analytics, Cybersecurity

Key Competitors: Infosys, Wipro, HCL Tech, Accenture, IBM, Cognizant

TCS is India's largest IT company and among the top 3 IT services companies globally.

sections.title: Vision & Mission
sections.content:
Vision:
'To be among the most valued technology companies in the world, recognized for innovation, quality, and customer-centricity.'

Mission:
'To help customers achieve their business objectives by providing innovative, best-in-class consulting, IT solutions and services. To make it a joy for all stakeholders to work with us.'

Strategic Goals:
- Be a top 10 global technology company
- Be the most admired employer globally
- Drive growth through AI, Cloud, and cognitive tech

TCS 4E Framework:
1. Empathy – Deep customer understanding
2. Execution Excellence – Consistent, flawless delivery
3. Ecosystem – Strong alliances with hyperscalers & academia
4. Energy – Sustainability and responsible business

Key Focus Areas:
- Cloud First (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
- AI & Cognitive (ignio™, Gen AI)
- Sustainability (Net Zero by 2030; Carbon Neutral since 2020)
- Agile/DevOps, Cybersecurity, Industry-specific solutions

TCS uses a 'Full Services' model — offering end-to-end IT, consulting, and BPS — positioning itself as a long-term transformation partner.

sections.title: Core Values
sections.content:
TCS's core values are rooted in the Tata Group's tradition of ethical business conduct.

Core Values (IRLEC):
1. Integrity – Ethical, transparent dealings; zero tolerance for corruption
2. Respect for the Individual – Diversity & inclusion; 150+ nationalities
3. Excellence – Quality in every deliverable; CMMI Level 5, ISO 9001, ISO 27001
4. Learning and Sharing – Continuous learning via Fresco Play, iEvolve, Xcelerate
5. Creativity and Innovation – R&D labs, ignio™, TCS BaNCS

Tata Code of Conduct:
- Applies to all TCS employees globally
- Covers: ethics, data privacy, anti-bribery, equal opportunity
- Acknowledged annually by all employees

Diversity & Inclusion:
- Women: ~35% of workforce
- TCS WoMen initiative supports women in leadership

Attrition:
- TCS maintains ~12–13% attrition — well below industry average of 20%+

sections.title: Business Segments
sections.content:
TCS organizes business across Industry Verticals and Service Lines.

Industry Verticals:
1. BFSI (~32%) — Largest; TCS BaNCS for core banking
2. Retail & Consumer — TCS OmniStore for omnichannel retail
3. CMT — Telecom OSS/BSS, 5G; HOBS platform
4. Manufacturing — Industry 4.0, smart manufacturing
5. Life Sciences & Healthcare — Clinical data, healthcare IT
6. Energy & Utilities — Smart grid, oil & gas IT
7. Others — Public sector, education, travel

Key Service Lines:
1. IT Services — App dev, testing, ERP (SAP, Oracle, Salesforce)
2. Consulting — Digital transformation, change management
3. BPS — Finance, HR outsourcing, customer lifecycle mgmt
4. Engineering — Product design, embedded systems, IoT
5. Cognitive Ops — ignio™ AI automation, RPA
6. Cloud — Multi-cloud, AWS/Azure/GCP, TCS Enterprise Cloud™
7. Cybersecurity — SOC, zero-trust, identity management
8. AI & Analytics — Gen AI, ML platforms, data engineering

Key Partnerships:
- Hyperscalers: AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud
- Enterprise Tech: SAP, Oracle, Salesforce, ServiceNow
- Academia: MIT, Carnegie Mellon, IITs, IIMs
- Sports: TCS NYC, Amsterdam, Toronto Marathons; TCS World 10K Bengaluru

sections.title: Key Milestones
sections.content:
1968 — TCS founded as Tata Sons division; F.C. Kohli becomes first GM
1969 — First software export to Burroughs Corp, USA
1971 — First overseas office in New York, USA
1981 — First dedicated software training center (Thiruvananthapuram)
1985 — First offshore development center established
1999 — ISO 9001 certified; $1 billion revenue milestone
2004 — IPO on BSE & NSE (largest Indian IPO at the time)
2007 — Acquired Citigroup Global Services (BPO)
2009 — N. Chandrasekaran appointed CEO & MD
2013 — First Indian IT company to cross $10 billion revenue
2017 — Rajesh Gopinathan appointed CEO & MD
2018 — TCS turns 50; first Indian company to hit $100B market cap
2020 — Achieved Carbon Neutrality; launched SBWS model (COVID-19)
2021 — Revenue crossed $25 billion
2023 — K. Krithivasan appointed CEO (June); revenue crossed $27B
2024 — Revenue ~$29.1B; headcount surpassed 6 lakh

Recognitions:
- Forbes Global 2000 | Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader
- Most Valuable Brand in India (Brand Finance)
- CMMI Level 5, ISO 27001, ISO 14001 certified
- Multiple NASSCOM awards

sections.title: TCS Products & Platforms
sections.content:
TCS has a strong portfolio of proprietary products and platforms.

1. TCS BaNCS — Core banking & financial services platform; used by banks, insurers, capital markets globally
2. ignio™ — AI/ML cognitive automation; self-healing IT, predictive analytics; flagship AI product
3. TCS iON — Cloud platform for education & assessments; powers large-scale exams (NTA, PSUs)
4. TCS MasterCraft — App modernization suite; legacy migration, code transformation (DevPlus, TransForm, DataPlus)
5. Quartz™ — Blockchain platform; cross-border payments, trade finance, smart contracts
6. TCS OmniStore — Unified retail commerce platform; POS, inventory, order mgmt
7. HOBS — Telecom BSS; order management & billing for telecom companies
8. TCS Enterprise Cloud™ — Multi-cloud mgmt & migration; FinOps, cloud-native dev
9. Optumera — AI-driven retail pricing, promotion & inventory optimization
10. TCS Cyber Security Suite — Threat intelligence, SOC, zero-trust security

Internal Platforms:
- Ultimatix — Employee portal (HR, payroll, timesheets, travel)
- Fresco Play — E-learning & certifications (earn Fresco Coins)
- iEvolve — Personalized skill development
- Xcelerate — Leadership & advanced learning
- SBWS — Secure Borderless Workspaces (remote work model)

sections.title: CSR & Sustainability
sections.content:
TCS deeply embeds CSR into its strategy, in line with the Tata Group's tradition of nation-building.

Sustainability:
- Carbon Neutral since 2020
- Net Zero target by 2030
- ISO 14001 (Environmental Mgmt), ISO 50001 (Energy Mgmt)
- Reports under GRI standards; member of UN Global Compact

Key CSR Programs:
1. BridgeIT — Digital literacy for rural students in India
2. goIT — STEM awareness for school students globally (USA, Canada, Europe)
3. Ignite My Future in School — Computational thinking for K–12 (USA); with Discovery Education
4. Adult Literacy Program — Basic literacy for rural adults via TCS iON
5. TCS IntelliPass — Career readiness & employability skills for youth
6. Purpose4Life — Employee volunteering platform; millions of hours annually
7. TCS Sustainathon — Global student competition to solve sustainability problems

Diversity:
- Women: ~35% of workforce
- TCS WoMen initiative for women in leadership
- Signatory to UN Women's Empowerment Principles

Sports Sponsorships:
- TCS NYC Marathon, Amsterdam Marathon, Toronto Waterfront Marathon, TCS World 10K Bengaluru

sections.title: IPA & TCS Programs
sections.content:
TCS IPA (iON Proctored Assessment) is the internal assessment for newly hired TCS employees.

What is IPA?
- Conducted for freshers joining through campus recruitment (NQT/Smart Hiring)
- Evaluates performance during initial training
- Two components: MCQ (objective) + Coding/Descriptive
- IPA score influences project allocation, designation, and first-year rating

Key Programs:
1. ILP (Initial Learning Program) — Flagship fresher training (3–6 months); covers Java, Python, C, SDLC, Agile, soft skills
2. TCS NQT — Entry test; sections: Numerical, Verbal, Reasoning, Programming Logic, Coding
3. Hiring Bands: Ninja (standard) → Digital (higher) → Prime (highest)
4. Fresco Play — E-learning; mandatory tracks by project; earn Fresco Coins
5. iEvolve — Personalized upskilling; linked to career growth
6. Xcelerate — Leadership development for mid/senior employees
7. SBWS (25x25) — By 2025, only 25% work from office at any time
8. Ultimatix — Central portal: timesheets, leave, payroll, performance
9. TCS GEMS — Peer/manager recognition; contributes to annual rating
10. TCS Digital Garage — Innovation hub for Gen AI, Metaverse, Quantum Computing

Career Path:
Trainee/ASE → Systems Engineer → IT Analyst → Assistant Consultant → Consultant → Lead/Principal Consultant → Architect → Senior Management

Performance Ratings:
A+ (Outstanding) > A (Excellent) > B (Good) > C (Satisfactory) > D (Needs Improvement)

Key Practices:
- Agile & SAFe | CMMI Level 5 | Six Sigma | ISO 27001, GDPR compliance

TCS Quick Facts:
- Founded: 1968 | IPO: 2004 | CEO: K. Krithivasan
- F.C. Kohli = 1st GM = Father of Indian IT
- BFSI = Largest segment | North America = Largest geography
- ignio = AI | BaNCS = Banking | iON = Assessments
- Carbon Neutral: 2020 | Net Zero target: 2030
- Listed on BSE & NSE only (NOT NYSE/LSE)
- Parent: Tata Sons | Employees: 6L+ | Countries: 55+ | Centers: ~150
